title: image-resizer OOMKills + latency after v1.9 deploy
alertText:
[PagerDuty] TRIGGERED · SEV2 · image-resizer
Pod restart storm + intermittent 5xx (datadog-monitor #50774)
OOMKilled = 14 in 12m · pod restarts = 14 · p99 latency spiking to ~1.8s on each restart
HTTP 5xx = 2.4% (transient, around restarts) · CPU nominal ~34%
Env: production · Region: us-west-2 · Deployment: image-resizer (limit 2Gi)
Correlated change: deploy image-resizer v1.9.0 @ 11:08 UTC
Ack: @oncall-media · Opened 11:14 UTC
dashboardSpec: Grafana 'image-resizer / runtime': container memory sawtooths up to the 2Gi limit and resets (each reset = an OOMKill restart); OOMKilled count steps up to 14; p99 latency spikes at each restart; CPU flat ~34% (resources otherwise nominal). Memory growth begins at the v1.9 deploy marker.
logs:
11:08:03  image-resizer  INFO   Rolling update started: v1.8.6 -> v1.9.0
11:08:31  image-resizer  INFO   Dependency upgraded: libvips 8.14 -> 8.16 (image decode/resize)
11:09:50  image-resizer  WARN   container memory 1.78Gi / 2Gi (89%) and climbing ~14MiB per 100 req
11:10:12  kubelet        WARN   Memory cgroup out of memory: Killed process (image-resizer), oom_score_adj
11:10:12  kubelet        WARN   Pod image-resizer-7c9 OOMKilled (reason: OOMKilled, exit 137) — restartCount=1
11:10:31  image-resizer  ERROR  GET /resize 502 (3 in-flight requests dropped on restart)
11:11:45  kubelet        WARN   Pod image-resizer-7c9 OOMKilled (exit 137) — restartCount=4
11:12:30  image-resizer  INFO   heap stable; native/off-heap memory grows monotonically with request count (libvips buffers not freed)
11:13:50  kubelet        WARN   Pod image-resizer-7c9 OOMKilled (exit 137) — restartCount=10
11:14:20  image-resizer  INFO   CPU 34%, request rate 220 rps (flat, no traffic spike)
11:15:02  image-resizer  WARN   OOMKilled total since 11:10 = 14
runbookMarkdown:
# image-resizer — On-call Runbook

## 1. OOMKill / restart storm
Symptoms: container memory climbing to the limit, `OOMKilled (exit 137)`, rising restartCount, latency spikes on each restart. If memory grows monotonically with request count and CPU/traffic are flat, suspect a memory leak introduced by a recent change. Fastest safe mitigation: `kubectl rollout undo deploy/image-resizer`. Stopgap only: raise the memory limit to buy time (does not fix the leak).

## 2. Native/off-heap leaks after a dependency bump
Image/codec libraries (libvips, ImageMagick) allocate off-heap buffers; a version bump can change buffer-freeing behavior and leak per request. Correlate the leak start with the deploy and the dependency delta, then roll the release back.

## 3. Traffic-driven saturation (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies when request rate spikes. Here rps is flat ~220 — not load.

## 4. CPU throttling (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies when CPU is pinned at the limit. Here CPU is ~34%.
groundTruthRootCause: Deploy v1.9.0 upgraded libvips 8.14 -> 8.16, which leaks off-heap (native) memory per resize request. Container memory climbs ~14MiB/100req to the 2Gi limit, the pod is OOMKilled (exit 137) and restarts — 14 times in 12 minutes — and each restart drops in-flight requests and spikes p99. Heap is stable and CPU/traffic are flat, so this is a native memory leak from the v1.9 dependency bump, not load or a heap issue.
expectedSlackUpdate: :rotating_light: SEV2 — image-resizer restart storm (us-west-2). 14 OOMKills/12m, p99 spiking to 1.8s since 11:08 UTC. Root cause: deploy v1.9 bumped libvips 8.14->8.16, leaking off-heap memory per request -> 2Gi limit -> OOMKilled restarts. Action: rolling back v1.9 (reversible). ETA ~5 min. Next update 11:30. IC: @oncall-media
